known_limitations: The required full-scope legacy-token scan reports unowned creation-flow files under frontend/src/components/listings because the broad command includes all listing components. I did not edit FinishedListingFlow.tsx or NewListingFlow.tsx because they are outside T8 write scope.
The initially requested unquoted zsh git diff --check invocation fails on the literal [id] route segment; the final check is run with quoted route paths so Git receives the intended files.

Browser QA was captured after the first implementation pass under .omo/evidence/dalya-listings-workspace-rebrand/task-8-browser-qa/. The live localhost route redirected unauthenticated traffic to /login, so the QA executor used a temporary frontend copy with local auth and API fulfillments to render the actual Next route surfaces at 1280, 768, and 375 widths.
adversarial_notes: Legacy dashboard knowledge/logistics/offers files were already redirects to /listings/[id]/* when read; I did not modify them.
The canonical /listings/[id] directory and several listing components were untracked before this task; I did not stage or clean them.
Knowledge document addition uses text-backed POST /api/v1/listings/{id}/documents, not binary upload, matching the supported endpoint in app/api/listings.py.
Seller offers endpoint currently returns pending offers derived from escalation reasons; the UI still supports past statuses for forward compatibility.

commands.result: 307 Temporary Redirect to /login with unauthenticated local server

commands.scenario: browser route rendering with local auth and API fulfillments
commands.result: PASS; screenshots, transcript, QA matrix, token scan, and cleanup receipt written

limitations: The screenshots use local Playwright API fulfillments because the real local route is authenticated and redirected unauthenticated requests to /login.
The browser QA pass preceded the review-blocker fix; the blocker fix was validated separately with typecheck, no-excuse, grep, and source inspection.

fixes.criterion: HIGH blocker: production-shaped knowledge missing/risk arrays must not crash React rendering
fixes.observable: KnowledgeSummary now models missing_information as string or {fact_group,label}, risk_flags as string or {fact_id,fact_key,label,value}; ListingKnowledgeSummaryPanel maps both arrays to Signal rows and renders item.label/item.detail, never the raw item object.

fixes.criterion: MEDIUM blocker: logistics form must not overwrite unsaved edits on React Query refetch
fixes.observable: ListingLogisticsForm tracks dirtyRef; data hydration exits while dirtyRef.current is true, marks dirty on section edits, resets on listingId changes, and clears dirty before successful save invalidation so server reloads remain sensible.

commands.invocation: for file in frontend/src/components/listings/ListingKnowledgeTypes.ts frontend/src/components/listings/ListingKnowledgeSummaryPanel.tsx frontend/src/components/viewings/ListingLogisticsForm.tsx; do printf '%s ' "$file"; awk '!/^[[:space:]]*$/ && !/^[[:space:]]*(\/\/|#|--)/' "$file" | wc -l; done

risks: Browser QA artifacts exist for the migrated route surfaces before the blocker fix. The blocker fix itself was validated with static/type checks focused on production-shaped knowledge summary items and logistics dirty-form hydration.
The broad legacy-token scan still reports out-of-scope creation-flow files under frontend/src/components/listings; those files were not edited.
